observed_signal: `crashsymbolication` keeps symbolication as copied Xcode/CLI handoff text and excludes command execution, directory search, symbol download, upload, mutation, and binary parsing.
surface_plugin_implication: Export handoff text by default, but keep raw symbolication output manual-review.

observed_signal: `dsymcatalog` owns explicit symbol metadata and path pointers while excluding dSYM/archive/app copying, scanning, mutation, upload, download, and DWARF/Mach-O parsing.
surface_plugin_implication: Export redacted catalog metadata only; exclude dSYM, archive, app bundle, app binary, and symbol-map contents by default.

source: Apple Xcode: Examining the fields in a crash report
observed_signal: Apple documents crash report sections such as process details, exception data, termination data, thread state, and binary images.
surface_plugin_implication: Raw crash reports can expose sensitive identifiers, paths, registers, frames, and binary metadata, so default them to manual-review.

source: Apple Xcode: Acquiring crash reports and diagnostic logs
observed_signal: Apple documents crash reports and diagnostic logs as explicit debugging artifacts obtained through Xcode and related workflows.
surface_plugin_implication: Surface should consume explicit crash artifacts only and should not collect logs or diagnostics during export.

observed_signal: Sentry's Apple SDK supports adding file or byte attachments such as logs or config files to event data visible alongside event details.
surface_plugin_implication: Surface should never auto-attach or upload crash files; third-party crash exports and attachments need manual review.

observed_signal: GitHub documents attaching files to issues and pull requests and notes that files are uploaded immediately when attached.
surface_plugin_implication: `diagnosticbundle` should copy issue/support summaries locally and never upload attachments automatically.

observed_signal: `BlockImageRenderer` currently creates an `NSHostingView`, asks for `bitmapImageRepForCachingDisplay`, caches display into that bitmap, and encodes PNG without explicit scale, appearance, or locale inputs.
surface_plugin_implication: Renderer scale/appearance/locale control belongs in the existing renderer owner, not a plugin or second preview renderer.

observed_signal: `baselineplatforms` says to prefer making `BlockImageRenderer` deterministic and to consider explicit renderer scale before adding platform lanes.
surface_plugin_implication: `rendererscalecontrol` should be a prerequisite to any second visual-baseline lane.

observed_signal: Apple documents creating an `NSBitmapImageRep` with explicit pixel width, pixel height, sample layout, color space, bytes per row, and bits per pixel.
surface_plugin_implication: Fixed renderer scale can be implemented by rendering into exact pixel dimensions rather than resizing output afterward.

observed_signal: Apple documents caching a view's display into an `NSBitmapImageRep`.
surface_plugin_implication: Surface should stay in the current AppKit rendering family while adding configuration.

observed_signal: Apple documents `NSAppearance` as the object representing standard interface appearances for AppKit rendering.
surface_plugin_implication: Preview rendering can set host appearance locally instead of mutating global app or system appearance.

observed_signal: SwiftUI exposes locale as an environment value for view rendering.
surface_plugin_implication: Preview rendering can set a deterministic locale such as `en_US_POSIX` without changing the user's global locale.

observed_signal: SwiftUI exposes color scheme as an environment value for rendering light or dark variants.
surface_plugin_implication: Preview rendering should pair explicit AppKit appearance with SwiftUI color scheme for deterministic light/dark baselines.

observed_signal: `diagnosticbundle` already defines `passthrough`, `summary-only`, `redact-known-keys`, `manual-review`, and `exclude`, plus deterministic `summary.md` and `redaction-report.json` output.
surface_plugin_implication: Redaction key policy should live inside `diagnosticbundle`, not a second sanitizer or registry.

observed_signal: `crashattachmentpolicy` marks raw crash reports, symbolication output, third-party crash exports, dSYM bundles, archives, app bundles, app binaries, and sysdiagnose artifacts as manual-review or excluded by default.
surface_plugin_implication: `diagnosticbundleredactionkeys` should refine structured summaries and metadata, not loosen crash/symbol artifact defaults.

source: OWASP Logging Cheat Sheet: Data to exclude
observed_signal: OWASP logging guidance calls out sensitive data classes that should be excluded or treated carefully, including session identifiers, access tokens, passwords, and personal data.
surface_plugin_implication: Surface diagnostic redaction should include fixed secret, identity, path, local-context, and crash-detail key classes.

observed_signal: Sentry recommends filtering or scrubbing sensitive data before it is sent or stored and highlights PII, authentication credentials, and confidential data.
surface_plugin_implication: Surface should redact structured exports before any manual sharing and should never rely on upload-time scrubbing.

observed_signal: Apple documents privacy handling for logged values that may be sensitive.
surface_plugin_implication: Diagnostic bundle summaries should avoid raw private log fields and keep text logs manual-review unless structured redaction is possible.

observed_signal: `visualbaselines` owns check/record commands, image comparison, diff artifact writing, and `.build/surface-status/visualbaselines.json`.
surface_plugin_implication: The consolidated report schema should live in the visual-baseline harness, not a plugin or second report registry.

observed_signal: `visualartifactretention` adds local retention, CI upload, baseline storage, size policy, and artifact-policy metadata to `visualbaselines.json`.
surface_plugin_implication: Artifact retention should be report fields read by CI, `previewgallery`, and `diagnosticbundle`, not a runtime artifact manager.

observed_signal: `baselineplatforms` adds platform lane metadata, runner label, OS/architecture/scale fields, warnings, and `platformMismatch` status.
surface_plugin_implication: Platform metadata and mismatch classification should be top-level report fields before pixel comparison.

observed_signal: `rendererscalecontrol` adds renderer configuration fields for scale policy, scale, appearance, color scheme, locale, and output pixel dimensions.
surface_plugin_implication: Renderer input/output metadata should be included in every visual baseline report before adding platform lanes.

observed_signal: JSON Schema defines a standard way to describe and validate JSON document structure.
surface_plugin_implication: Surface can define an exact v1 report contract and later add a schema file without creating a schema registry.

observed_signal: Playwright warns that browser rendering can vary by host OS, version, settings, hardware, power source, headless mode, and other factors, and recommends consistent environments.
surface_plugin_implication: `visualbaselines.json` should record platform and renderer metadata so wrong-lane runs are separated from UI pixel changes.

observed_signal: Coast exposes local screen recording sessions, top apps/domains, full-text OCR search, representative frame samples, frame metadata/OCR/images, current screen capture, and optional AX trees.
surface_plugin_implication: `activitycontext` should show bounded current/recent context and avoid becoming a timeline browser, OCR search UI, or permission manager.

observed_signal: Cued exposes local contacts, conversations, messages, unread counts, and relationship patterns such as follow-up needed and unread triage through the encrypted local datastore.
surface_plugin_implication: `followupqueue` should read local Cued rows and show who needs attention without sending messages, enriching contacts, or duplicating Cued's relationship graph.

observed_signal: The build note selects `activitycontext`, `followupqueue`, and `githubqueue` and records their fixture-first, background-live-adapter boundaries.
surface_plugin_implication: These three plugins are now implemented as BlockRuntime targets and should remain inside the existing registry/runtime path.
